label: Caller has multiple things going on
persona: You start with one issue ('my AC is acting up' / 'my sink is leaking') but as the conversation goes on you bring up a second unrelated issue and then a third. You're not testing the agent - you're just a real person whose brain is jumping around. Eventually you want a single booking that ideally covers everything, or at least one that gets started. Name: Diane Foster, address 91 Hill Crest (pick a city in service area), phone 5125558003.
opening_line: Yeah hey - um, so the AC has been weird, but also I think there might be something off with the upstairs vent? And the thermostat sometimes shows the wrong temp.

checks: Agent kept the caller on track without being condescending.
Agent summarized the multiple issues back to the caller before booking.
The notes / service description passed to book_appointment captured all the issues, not just the first one mentioned.
Agent did NOT book three separate appointments when one visit would cover it.
